---
title: "Design system — internal styleguide"
subtitle: "Not learner content. Renders every Larnix UI component for review + the a11y gate."
search: false
---

::: {.callout-warning}
This page is an **internal styleguide**, not a chapter. It exists so the design
system can be eyeballed and so the accessibility gate (alt-text + colour
contrast) has a single page that exercises every component. Toggle the
light/dark switch in the navbar to check both themes.
:::

## Difficulty badges

A chapter's tier, shown as a labelled pill (the colour is reinforced by text, so
meaning never depends on colour alone):

- {{< badge difficulty=beginner >}}
- {{< badge difficulty=intermediate >}}
- {{< badge difficulty=advanced >}}

## Compute badges

Where a chapter runs:

- {{< badge compute=browser >}} — runs in the browser, zero install, ₹0
- {{< badge compute=colab >}} — opens in Google Colab (free tier)
- {{< badge compute=gpu >}} — needs a GPU session

## Status badges

How fresh the content is:

- {{< badge status=stable >}}
- {{< badge status=frontier >}}

## A badge row (as it appears under a chapter title)

{{< badge difficulty=beginner >}} {{< badge compute=browser >}} {{< badge status=stable >}}

## Key Takeaways box

Authored as a fenced div with class `key-takeaways`:

::: {.key-takeaways}
### Key Takeaways

1. The Key Takeaways box is the one block a learner can re-read to recall the
   whole chapter.
2. It uses the brand accent and a left border so it stands out from notes/tips.
3. Authors write `::: {.key-takeaways}` and a numbered list inside.
:::

## Standard callouts (for reference)

::: {.callout-tip}
Tip — a practical shortcut.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Watch out — a common mistake.
:::
